title: GVN: Inhibit PRE for llvm.protected.field.ptr.
author: pcc

tests.test_body:
; Check that PRE is inhibited for llvm.protected.field.ptr.
declare void @use(ptr)

define void @test1(i1 %c, ptr %arg) {
;
  br i1 %c, label %bb1, label %bb2

bb1:
  %call = call ptr @llvm.protected.field.ptr.p0(ptr %arg, i64 1, i1 true)
  br label %bb3

bb2:
  %call2 = call ptr @llvm.protected.field.ptr.p0(ptr %arg, i64 1, i1 true)
  %v = load ptr, ptr %call2
  call void @use(ptr %v)
  br label %bb3

bb3:
  %call3 = call ptr @llvm.protected.field.ptr.p0(ptr %arg, i64 1, i1 true)
  %v2 = load ptr, ptr %call3
  call void @use(ptr %v2)
  br label %bb2
}

comments.author: pcc
comments.body:
There may be a similar issue with PRE operating on swifterror values (which we already forbid across phis).

As an alternative to this change, we may consider moving the check to a function and calling it from canReplaceOperandWithVariable, similar to what I did in the [original version of #151649](https://github.com/pcc/llvm-project/commit/e4a29e103bf07620729af865f78ee95aa1043e23). This should also fix the potential swifterror issue.
